back_translation: 'I have made a covenant with my eyes; how then could I gaze with desire at a virgin?
For what is the portion appointed by God from above, or the heritage from the Almighty on high?
Does not disaster come to the unjust and calamity to those who do evil?
Does He not see my ways and count my every step?
If I have walked in falsehood or my foot has hurried to deceit,
let God weigh me on honest scales, that He may know my integrity.
If my steps have turned from the path, if my heart has followed my eyes, or if any impurity has clung to my hands,
then let another eat what I have sown, and let my crops be uprooted.
If my heart has been enticed by my neighbor's wife, or I have lurked at his door,
then let my wife grind grain for another, and let other men sleep with her.
For that would be a heinous crime, a sin to be judged.
For it is a fire that burns down to Abaddon, and it would burn all my harvest to the root.
If I have denied justice to my manservant or maidservant when they complained against me,
what will I do when God rises to judge? When He calls me to account, how will I answer Him?
Did not He who made me in the womb also make him? Did not the same One form us both in the womb?
If I have withheld what the poor desired, or made the eyes of the widow grow weary with despair,
if I have eaten my food alone, without sharing it with the fatherless—
but from my youth I reared the orphan like a father, and from my mother's womb I guided the widow—
if I have seen anyone perishing for lack of clothing, or a needy man without a covering,
if his heart did not bless me when I warmed him with the fleece of my sheep,
if I have raised my hand against the fatherless because I saw I had support at the gate,
then let my arm fall from my shoulder and be broken off at the joint.
For calamity from God terrifies me, and I cannot withstand His splendor.
If I have put my trust in gold or said to fine gold, 'You are my security,'
if I have rejoiced because of my great wealth, because my hand had gained so much,
if I have gazed at the sun shining brightly or the moon moving in splendor,
so that my heart was secretly enticed and my hand threw a kiss from my mouth,
that too would be a sin to be judged, for I would have denied God on high.
If I have rejoiced at my enemy's ruin or exulted when evil befell him—
I have never let my mouth sin by asking for his life with a curse—
if the men of my household have not said, 'Who is there who has not been filled with the meat Job provides?'—
no stranger had to lodge in the street, for my door was always open to the traveler—
if I have covered my transgressions like Adam, hiding my guilt in my heart,
because I greatly feared the crowd and the contempt of the clans terrified me, so that I kept silent and dared not go outside—
Oh, that someone would hear me! Behold, here is my signature; let the Almighty answer me, and let my accuser write out his indictment.
Surely I would carry it on my shoulder and wear it like a crown.
I would give an account of all my steps and approach Him like a prince.
If my land cries out against me and its furrows weep together,
if I have eaten its produce without payment or caused the death of its tenants,
then let thorns grow instead of wheat and stinkweed instead of barley.' The words of Job are ended.
